> x402

# x402 — HTTP Payments Protocol

A payments protocol for the internet, built on HTTP. Enables machine-to-machine payments via HTTP 402 responses. Multi-language SDK (TypeScript, Python, Go, Java) with shared protocol specifications. Apache-2.0 license.

## Supported Chains

Base, Solana, Polygon, Avalanche, Stellar, Aptos, SKALE, Sei, plus testnets.

## Transports

- **HTTP 402** — Primary; servers return `402 Payment Required` with payment headers
- **MCP** — Model Context Protocol transport for AI agents
- **A2A** — Agent-to-Agent transport

## Payment Schemes

- **exact** — Stable; fixed price per request
- **upto** — In-progress; maximum budget with actual usage billing

## Directory Structure

```
x402/
├── typescript/          # TypeScript SDK (reference implementation)
│   ├── packages/
│   │   ├── core/        # @x402/core — protocol primitives, facilitator
│   │   ├── extensions/  # @x402/extensions — protocol extensions
│   │   ├── http/        # @x402/http — HTTP 402 transport
│   │   ├── legacy/      # Legacy compatibility layer
│   │   ├── mcp/         # @x402/mcp — MCP transport
│   │   └── mechanisms/  # @x402/mechanisms — chain-specific payment logic
│   ├── site/            # Documentation site
│   └── turbo.json       # Turborepo pipeline config
├── python/              # Python SDK
│   ├── x402/            # Main package (client.py, server.py, facilitator.py)
│   └── legacy/          # Legacy compatibility
├── go/                  # Go SDK (module: github.com/coinbase/x402/go)
│   ├── client.go, server.go, facilitator.go       # Core interfaces
│   ├── client_hooks.go, server_hooks.go, facilitator_hooks.go  # Lifecycle hooks
│   ├── http/            # HTTP transport (gin/, nethttp/)
│   ├── mcp/             # MCP transport
│   ├── mechanisms/      # Payment mechanisms (evm/, svm/)
│   ├── extensions/      # Protocol extensions
│   └── signers/         # Cryptographic signer implementations
├── java/                # Java SDK (Maven)
├── specs/               # Protocol specifications (authoritative)
│   ├── x402-specification-v1.md
│   ├── x402-specification-v2.md
│   ├── schemes/         # exact, upto
│   ├── transports-v1/
│   ├── transports-v2/   # http.md, mcp.md, a2a.md
│   └── extensions/      # Extension specs
├── examples/            # Example implementations (typescript/, python/, go/)
├── e2e/                 # End-to-end tests
├── contracts/           # EVM contracts (Foundry)
├── docs/                # Mintlify documentation site (MDX)
│   └── AGENTS.md        # Docs-specific agent instructions
├── static/              # Static assets (diagrams/images)
├── CONTRIBUTING.md      # Contribution guide
├── PROJECT-IDEAS.md     # Grant/project ideas
├── ROADMAP.md           # Roadmap
└── SECURITY.md          # Security policy
```

## Key Specification Files

- `specs/x402-specification-v2.md` — Current protocol specification
- `specs/transports-v2/http.md` — HTTP transport spec
- `specs/transports-v2/mcp.md` — MCP transport spec
- `specs/transports-v2/a2a.md` — A2A transport spec
- `specs/schemes/exact/` — Exact payment scheme
- `specs/schemes/upto/` — Upto payment scheme (in-progress)
- `specs/extensions/` — All extension specifications

## Build, Test, and Lint Commands

### TypeScript (reference SDK)

Requires pnpm@10.7.0 and Node.js.

```bash
cd typescript
pnpm install              # Install all workspace dependencies
pnpm build                # Build all packages (via Turborepo)
pnpm test                 # Run all tests (vitest)
pnpm lint                 # Lint all packages (eslint)
pnpm format:check         # Check formatting (prettier)
pnpm format               # Auto-format code
```

Individual packages: `cd packages/core && pnpm test`, etc.

### Python

Requires `uv` for dependency management.

```bash
cd python
uv sync                   # Install dependencies
uv run pytest             # Run tests
```

### Go

Requires Go 1.24+.

```bash
cd go
go build ./...            # Build all packages
go test ./...             # Run all tests
golangci-lint run         # Lint
```

### Java

Requires Maven and JDK.

```bash
cd java
mvn clean install         # Build, lint (checkstyle + spotbugs), and test
```

### EVM Contracts

Requires Foundry (forge).

```bash
cd contracts/evm
forge build               # Compile contracts
forge test                # Run tests
```

### End-to-End Tests

```bash
cd e2e
pnpm install && pnpm test
```

## Code Style Conventions

- **TypeScript**: eslint + prettier enforced in CI. Configs in each package. Changeset-based versioning — run `pnpm changeset` when changing public APIs.
- **Python**: Follow standard Python conventions. `uv` for dependency management.
- **Go**: golangci-lint enforced in CI. Changelog entries via `changie`. Follow standard Go formatting (`gofmt`).
- **Java**: checkstyle + spotbugs enforced via Maven. No stable release yet (SNAPSHOT only).
- All SDKs: tests required for new functionality. Documentation updates required alongside code changes.

## Contribution Workflow

1. Fork and create a branch: `feature/your-feature-name`
2. Implement changes with tests and documentation
3. For TypeScript: run `pnpm changeset` if changing public APIs
4. For Go: use `changie new` to add a changelog entry
5. Ensure all linting and tests pass locally
6. Open a PR against `main` — PRs are reviewed before merge; never commit directly to main

## Multi-Language Notes

- **TypeScript is the reference implementation** — protocol changes land here first, then are ported to Python, Go, and Java.
- Protocol specifications in `specs/` are authoritative. All SDKs must conform to them.
- Each SDK has a `legacy/` package for backward compatibility — do not modify these.
- The `docs/AGENTS.md` file contains instructions scoped to the Mintlify documentation site only.
- CI runs checks for all SDKs independently — a failure in one does not block others.
- Each SDK manages its own versioning (TypeScript: changesets, Go: changie, Python/Java: respective tooling).
